channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Hackers steal medical records and financial data from 1.2M patients in massive healthcare breach
item.description: A cyberattack on SimonMed Imaging exposed personal information of 1.2 million patients, including medical records, financial details and identity papers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The hot issues for CIOs at TechCrunch Disrupt 2025
item.description: The TechCrunch Disrupt conference has long provided valuable insights to and about startups and their investors — but CIOs can learn by talking with startups too, and there’s plenty to learn about at this year’s event.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google TPUs find sweet spot of AI demand, a decade after chip’s debut
item.description: In an AI chip industry that's almost entirely commanded by Nvidia Corp., a Google chip first developed more than 10 years ago especially for artificial intelligence tasks is finally gaining momentum outside its home company as a way to train and run complex AI models.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Intel rallies after forecast signals a comeback Is underway
item.description: Intel Corp. shares jumped after the chipmaker returned to profitability and gave an upbeat revenue forecast, suggesting that it's making progress on a long and challenging comeback attempt.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Lego just announced its official Black Friday sale - here are 11 early deals I'd buy right now
item.description: Lego’s own Black Friday deals event is officially returning for 2025, and with it, you can expect major discounts on sets that only Lego sells – but in the lead-up, you can save on these 12 deals from Lego and other retailers.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Krol, TechRadar

item.title: In a California farming region, researchers are mapping rural heat to protect farmworkers
item.description: In the summers, the sky is jet black when Raul Cruz arrives at this Imperial Valley sugarcane field to start his day. He chops, cleans and bundles the crop, taking heed as the sun rises. It's hard work, but so is starting at 4 a.m.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and JAE C. HONG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The iPhone 18 Pro could come with a big Starlink upgrade – and it sounds perfect for off-grid adventures
item.description: We're starting to hear a few rumors around what Apple may have planned for the next set of flagship iPhones.
item.dc:creator: David Nield, TechRadar

item.title: EU accuses Meta and TikTok of breaching transparency rules
item.description: The European Union says Meta and TikTok have breached transparency obligations under the Digital Services Act.
item.dc:creator: Sam Mcneil, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Microsoft has allegedly tasked Xbox to deliver a 30% profit margin, and if true, it could explain the reason behind its price hikes and mass layoffs
item.description: Xbox's job cuts and price increases could be due to a push from Microsoft to achieve a 30% profit margin.
item.dc:creator: Rhys Wood, TechRadar

item.title: Turning vision into value: The strategic role of intelligent IP cameras
item.description: The cameras and sensors that are installed across your business are doing more than watching. These IP cameras serve as a network of intelligent eyes across your operations, providing a potentially untapped resource that could improve business intelligence and operational efficiencies.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Python has a friend in Rust
item.description: Python 3.14 is here, and it’s bursting with goodies! Plus, Rust is making it easier to redistribute Python apps, PDM is simplifying Python package management, and Java is challenging Python for developing AI applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Repsol lanza su plataforma de multiagentes de IA
item.description: Repsol ha puesto en marcha su propia plataforma de construcción de agentes inteligentes, en colaboración con Accenture, que facilita la configuración de agentes de inteligencia artificial que se organizan en flujos de trabajo.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft debuts Mico, Copilot’s AI alternative to Clippy
item.description: Clippy, the animated paper clip that annoyed Microsoft Office users nearly three decades ago, might have just been ahead of its time.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Rivian cuts about 600 jobs in latest setback for EV maker
item.description: Rivian Automotive Inc. is cutting more than 600 jobs, or about 4.5% of its workforce, as the company contends with an unsteady market for electric vehicles in the US.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Musk's rollercoaster year: From boycotts and plunging earnings to a potential trillion-dollar payday
item.description: Elon Musk literally left a government job with a black eye, has seen profits at his car company sink and must worry about federal regulators disrupting his plans to unleash a fleet of driverless taxis on America's roads in the next few years.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Condon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: AWS reveals new cloud incident reporting tool, with almost zero sense of irony
item.description: AWS CloudWatch will get new incident report generation tool - days after an AWS outage affected parts of the internet.
item.dc:creator: Craig Hale, TechRadar

item.title: Trump calls off plans to deploy federal troops to San Francisco
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he would not deploy federal troops to San Francisco, reversing course on plans to implement anticrime and immigration enforcement in the Bay Area.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, The Hill

item.title: Celtic vs Sturm Graz live stream – How to watch Europa League online, FREE trial, kick-off time
item.description: Celtic take on Sturm Graz: European night sparks, Austrian counter-press chaos, and Kyogo Furuhashi magic — who will roar the loudest in the Europa League 2025?
item.dc:creator: Tom Bailey, TechRadar

item.title: Living in giant moon glass spheres could be our future
item.description: California company Skyeports creates self-healing glass spheres from Moon regolith that generate solar power and support plant growth for sustainable lunar living.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Extreme brings AI agent to Platform ONE for swift network diagnostics, troubleshooting
item.description: Extreme Networks has officially worked its Service AI Agent into its flagship network management package, Platform ONE, to give customers rapid access to diagnostics, autonomous troubleshooting, root-cause analysis, dashboards, and automation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google’s Quantum chip claims 13,000x speed advantage over supercomputers
item.description: Google Quantum AI has demonstrated what it describes as a verifiable quantum advantage — running a new algorithm on quantum hardware that outperforms classical supercomputers by a factor of 13,000.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: What Americans think about the environmental impact of AI, according to a new poll
item.description: A new poll finds that as the United States rapidly builds massive data centers for the development of artificial intelligence, many Americans are concerned about the environmental impact.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Mcdermott And Linley Sanders, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Agentic AI – Ongoing coverage of its impact on the enterprise
item.description: Over the next few years, agentic AI is expected to bring not only rapid technological breakthroughs, but a societal transformation, redefining how we live, work and interact with the world. And this shift is happening quickly.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: American e-waste is causing a 'hidden tsunami' in Southeast Asia, report says
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Millions of tons of discarded electronics from the United States are being shipped overseas, much of it to developing countries in Southeast Asia unprepared to safely handle hazardous waste, according to a new report released Wednesday by an environmental watchdog.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: AI helps IT teams resolve incidents faster, report finds
item.description: New data suggests that generative artificial intelligence (genAI) is delivering measurable benefits for IT organizations and their IT Service Management (ITSM) efforts.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Schiff presses Witkoff on divesting from Trump-tied crypto venture
item.description: Sen. Adam Schiff (D-Calif.) and seven other Senate Democrats pressed U.S. special envoy Steve Witkoff on Wednesday about divesting his stake in World Liberty Financial, the crypto venture launched by the Trump and Witkoff families last year.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: AWS DNS error hits DynamoDB, causing problems for multiple services and customers
item.description: Monday got off to a bad start for Amazon Web Services users served by the company’s US-EAST-1 region, when a DNS problem rendered the DynamoDB API unreliable, with consequences for many AWS services and customers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: China accuses US of cyberattack on national time center
item.description: China said any damage to related facilities could have disrupted network communications, financial systems and power supply.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
